project: Wuxia Life P1 Event Quality and Rhythm
description: Improve the core event system after P0 by making event rhythm observable and controlled, introducing event content quality gates, standardizing event/effect formats, and replacing dynamic condition expression execution with a controlled evaluator.

userStories.title: Confirm P0 Baseline
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want to confirm the P0 baseline before P1 implementation so that P1 does not build on unfinished remediation work.
acceptanceCriteria: P0 PRD and P0 delivery evidence or implementation notes are reviewed
P0 entry status is recorded as complete, partial, waived, or blocked
Affected P1 stories are identified if P0 evidence is incomplete
No business code is changed
userStories.notes: P0 baseline reviewed against docs/PRD/p0-core-experience-remediation.prd.json and docs/test-reports/p0-delivery-notes.md. Entry status: complete. Affected P1 stories: none.

userStories.title: Map Event Selection Flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the full event selection flow documented so that rhythm changes have clear and safe implementation points.
acceptanceCriteria: Candidate loading, age filtering, condition filtering, cooldown, storyline checks, daily fallback, weighting, annual pressure, and trigger recording are documented in order
Each step lists inputs, outputs, and fallback behavior
Formal event and daily event switch conditions are identified
A flow description or diagram is produced
userStories.notes: Documented end-to-end flow in docs/test-reports/us-002-map-event-selection-flow.md, including ordered formal selection stages, daily fallback switches, annual pressure behavior, weighted pick factors, and post-selection trigger recording points.

userStories.title: Define Rhythm Metrics
userStories.description: As a designer, I want event rhythm to be measurable so that rhythm improvements can be evaluated with evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Metrics include event count per age, empty ages, formal event ratio, daily event ratio, choice event ratio, storyline continuity, and critical event delay
Metrics include target ranges or P1 observation baselines
Metrics can be associated with simulation samples
userStories.notes: Implemented `scripts/reportRhythmMetrics.ts` and `npm run report:rhythm-metrics` to output event count per age, empty ages, formal/daily/choice ratios, storyline continuity, and critical event delay with sample association (`sampleId`, `seed`, `maxAge`) plus P1 observation baselines. Metrics and baseline usage documented in docs/test-reports/us-003-define-rhythm-metrics.md.

userStories.title: Create Rhythm Baseline Report
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a current event rhythm baseline so that future changes can be compared against it.
acceptanceCriteria: A fixed seed or fixed simulation input generates the baseline
The report covers a complete life sample or representative age bands
The report outputs the P1 rhythm metrics
The report can be regenerated with a local command
userStories.notes: Added deterministic baseline report generator `npm run report:rhythm-baseline` (`scripts/generateRhythmBaselineReport.ts`) with fixed seed/maxAge inputs, complete life sample (`maxAge=80`), representative age-band summary, and embedded P1 rhythm metrics output. Baseline artifact generated at `docs/test-reports/us-004-rhythm-baseline.md`.

userStories.title: Design Layered Event Selection
userStories.description: As a designer, I want critical, storyline, formal, daily, and fallback events to have clear selection boundaries so that events are not randomly stuffed into the life flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Event layers are defined: critical, storyline, regular formal, daily, and fallback
Entry and fallback conditions are defined for each layer
Skippable and protected event types are identified
No large-scale data rewrite is required for the design
userStories.notes: Defined a five-layer selection strategy (critical, storyline, regular formal, daily, fallback) with explicit entry/fallback rules, skippable vs protected event boundaries, and no-large-data-rewrite constraint in docs/test-reports/us-005-layered-event-selection-design.md.

userStories.title: Implement Low-Risk Rhythm Adjustment
userStories.description: As a player, I want event pacing to be steadier so that the life simulation has rhythm instead of event pileups or idle gaps.
acceptanceCriteria: Selection logic follows the approved layered strategy
Critical events remain triggerable
Daily events intervene only when formal events are insufficient or pacing requires them
Rhythm baseline report shows the metric changes
Default test gate passes
userStories.notes: Implemented layered low-risk selection orchestration in `GameEngineIntegration.selectEvent` (critical -> storyline -> regular formal -> daily -> fallback) while preserving existing weighting, cooldown, and repetition controls. Added regression coverage for daily fallback boundary and critical-lane protection in `tests/AllTests.ts`. Regenerated baseline report (`npm run report:rhythm-baseline`) to capture post-change metrics. Validation: `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` passed.

userStories.title: Add Rhythm Regression Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want regression tests for event rhythm so that future weight changes do not silently break pacing.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover empty candidate fallback, critical event protection, daily event intervention, and weighted candidate selection
Tests use deterministic inputs or fixed random seeds
Default test gate passes
userStories.notes: Added deterministic rhythm regression coverage in `tests/AllTests.ts` for empty-candidate daily fallback, critical-lane protection under rhythm pause, daily intervention when only regular formal events are available and paused, and weighted candidate selection with fixed random input/weights. Validation: `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` passed.

userStories.title: Inventory Event Quality Problems
userStories.description: As a content maintainer, I want known event quality problem types inventoried so that gates target real content risks.
acceptanceCriteria: Inventory covers duplicate titles, duplicate IDs, invalid age ranges, empty content, empty effects, invalid conditions, invalid stats, same-age congestion, broken storylines, and unreachable events
Each problem type has a proposed severity
No event data is modified
userStories.notes: Documented event quality problem inventory in `docs/test-reports/us-008-event-quality-problem-inventory.md`, covering duplicate titles, duplicate IDs, invalid age ranges, empty content, empty effects, invalid conditions, invalid stats, same-age congestion, broken storylines, and unreachable events; each type includes a proposed severity. This is a read-only documentation change and does not modify event data.

userStories.title: Define Event Quality Gate Rules
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want clear event quality gate rules so that new content is not validated by manual inspection alone.
acceptanceCriteria: Severity levels blocker, major, and minor are defined
Blocking issues are explicitly listed
Warning-only issues are explicitly listed
Rules are documented or encoded in validation configuration
userStories.notes: Defined blocker/major/minor severity model and explicit blocking vs warning-only issue lists in `docs/test-reports/us-009-event-quality-gate-rules.md`, and encoded the same policy in reusable validation config `scripts/eventQualityGateRules.ts` for follow-up validation script implementation.

userStories.title: Implement Event Quality Validation Script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a command that scans event data and outputs structured issues so that content problems are repeatably detectable.
acceptanceCriteria: The script scans event data that is actually loaded by the event system
Output includes event ID, source, issue type, severity, and explanation
Blocker issues cause the command to fail
The script runs without starting the frontend
userStories.notes: Added `scripts/validateEventQuality.ts` and command `npm run validate:event-quality` to scan the event set loaded through `EventLoader` and emit structured issue objects with `eventId`, `source`, `issueType`, `severity`, and `explanation`. The script reuses `scripts/eventQualityGateRules.ts` as severity/blocking source of truth, runs headless in Node, and exits non-zero when blocker issues are found.

userStories.title: Wire Event Quality Gate into Validation
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want event quality validation to be part of an explicit verification flow so that content quality is release-visible.
acceptanceCriteria: A command name for event quality validation is documented
Documentation explains when to run the command
If wired into default validation, current baseline passes or has an approved waiver list
Default test gate passes
userStories.notes: Wired `npm run validate:event-quality` into the default validation command (`npm run validate`) and CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) so event content quality is release-visible. Updated `docs/release-validation-contract.md` to document command name, execution order, and run timing (event content/metadata/condition/effect changes, plus default gate/CI). Baseline validation passed after wiring: `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, `npm run validate:event-quality`, and full `npm run validate`.

userStories.title: Inventory Event and Effect Formats
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to know every existing event and effect field style before standardizing formats.
acceptanceCriteria: Inventory covers top-level event fields, content fields, autoEffects, effects, choice.effects, choice.outcomes, requirements, thresholds, and triggerConditions
Usage count is reported for each format
Each format is classified as main-flow, legacy-compatible, or suspected-deprecated
No event data is modified
userStories.notes: Implemented `scripts/inventoryEventEffectFormats.ts` and `npm run report:event-format-inventory` to inventory format usage across top-level event fields, content fields, autoEffects, effects, choice.effects, choice.outcomes, requirements, thresholds, and triggerConditions with per-format usage counts and classification (`main-flow`/`legacy-compatible`/`suspected-deprecated`). Generated read-only artifact `docs/test-reports/us-012-event-effect-format-inventory.md`; no event data was modified.

userStories.title: Define Target Event Format
userStories.description: As a content author, I want one recommended event and effect format so that future content has a single correct shape.
acceptanceCriteria: Target event structure is defined
Target effect structure is defined
Choice and auto event standard forms are defined
Deprecated fields and migration window are documented
The standard is written into documentation
userStories.notes: Defined canonical event/effect authoring standard in `docs/test-reports/us-013-target-event-format-standard.md`, including target event structure, canonical effect schema (`type + target + value + operator`), standard forms for auto/choice events, deprecated legacy aliases (`stat`/`flag`/`event`, `content.autoEffects`, broad `triggerConditions`), and a documented migration window.

userStories.title: Add Format Validation and Migration Hints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want old and mixed formats to be detected with migration hints so that format unification can proceed safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation distinguishes target, legacy, and mixed formats
Legacy formats produce migration hints
Dangerous mixed formats produce blocker or major issues
No large automatic event rewrite is performed
userStories.notes: Extended `scripts/validateEventQuality.ts` to classify every loaded event as `target`/`legacy`/`mixed`, emit migration hints for legacy fields, and raise dedicated severity-based issues for mixed formats (`mixed_format_detected` major, `mixed_format_dangerous` blocker for ambiguous dual-shape cases). Added format issue taxonomy in `scripts/eventQualityGateRules.ts` and output-level `formatSummary` + `formatByEventId` for safe migration tracking without automatic data rewrites. Validation: `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, and `npm run validate:event-quality` passed.

userStories.title: Migrate Low-Risk Event Format Samples
userStories.description: As a content maintainer, I want a small low-risk sample migration so that the target format is proven before broad migration.
acceptanceCriteria: A small set of low-risk events is selected
Migrated events remain behaviorally equivalent
Relevant format validation passes
Default test gate passes
userStories.notes: Selected low-risk sample `career_good_evil_war` in `src/data/lines/middle-age-career.json` and migrated legacy `triggerConditions` to canonical expression `conditions` while keeping age-range gating untouched. Added regression coverage in `tests/AllTests.ts` to verify trigger behavior remains equivalent (requires `is_sect_leader` flag). Validation passed: `npm run validate:event-quality` (sample now classified as `target`), `npm run typecheck`, and `npm test`.

userStories.title: Define Condition Expression Grammar
userStories.description: As a developer, I want condition expression syntax to be explicit so that the evaluator can be safe, testable, and diagnosable.
acceptanceCriteria: Supported property access, flag queries, event queries, comparison operators, logic operators, and parentheses are defined
Unsupported syntax is listed
Invalid expression error behavior is defined
The grammar is documented or reflected in types
userStories.notes: Defined explicit P1 condition expression grammar in `docs/test-reports/us-016-condition-expression-grammar.md` and added type-level grammar contract in `src/types/conditionExpression.ts` (supported access/query/operators/parentheses, unsupported syntax list, and structured invalid-expression error behavior via `ConditionExpressionError` codes). Wired `EventCondition.expression` to `ConditionExpression` for type-level reflection.

userStories.title: Implement Controlled Condition Evaluator
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want expression conditions evaluated without dynamic function execution so that event conditions are safer and more stable.
acceptanceCriteria: The evaluator covers the approved P1 expression grammar
The evaluator does not use dynamic function execution for expressions
Errors include the expression and a useful reason
Existing legal expressions pass tests
userStories.notes: Replaced expression execution in `ConditionEvaluator` with a controlled tokenizer + recursive-descent parser/evaluator covering P1 grammar constructs (player property access, direct player properties, `flags.has(...)`, `events.has(...)`, comparison/logical operators, and parentheses) with no dynamic function execution. Added fail-close diagnostics that include the original expression and parse reason, plus regression tests for legal grammar combinations and invalid-expression error logging. Validation: `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` passed.

userStories.title: Replace Runtime Condition Evaluation
userStories.description: As a player, I want event and choice conditions to use one reliable evaluator so that story triggering is consistent and explainable.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime event conditions use the controlled evaluator
Choice conditions, thresholds, and trigger conditions have clear responsibility boundaries
Failed conditions do not silently trigger events
Default test gate passes
userStories.notes: Unified runtime condition gating in `GameEngineIntegration` via a single guard path (`conditions` + `thresholds` + legacy `triggerConditions`) and fail-close behavior. Choice/outcome runtime now rejects function-form conditions and relies on core evaluator entry to avoid split condition execution paths. Added regression tests for legacy `triggerConditions` filtering and function-condition fail-close fallback behavior. Validation: `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` passed.

userStories.title: Replace Dynamic Evaluation in Tests and Simulators
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests and simulation tools to reuse runtime condition evaluation so that validation matches gameplay.
acceptanceCriteria: Test framework and simulator condition checks reuse runtime evaluation
No independent dynamic function evaluation branch remains in tests or simulators
Simulation reports still generate successfully
Default test gate passes
userStories.notes: Removed simulator-local dynamic expression evaluation in `tests/GameProcessSimulator.ts` and routed choice-outcome condition checks through runtime `gameEngine.isChoiceAvailable`, reusing `ConditionEvaluator` to align test/simulator behavior with gameplay. Eliminated `new Function` condition execution branch from test simulator path while preserving report generation flow through the default simulation suite. Validation: `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` passed (`testGameSimulation` report generation included).

userStories.title: Add Condition Security and Regression Tests
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want legal, illegal, and malicious expression tests so that condition evaluation cannot regress to unsafe behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover legal expressions, invalid fields, invalid syntax, flag queries, event queries, logic composition, and parentheses
Tests cover malicious or non-whitelisted syntax not being executed
Default test gate passes
userStories.notes: Extended condition evaluator regression coverage in `tests/AllTests.ts` for legal grammar baseline plus fail-close checks on invalid fields, invalid syntax, non-string `flags.has` argument, and malicious/non-whitelisted payloads (global access, assignment, constructor chain, and function-call forms) with explicit side-effect guard assertion. Validation passed: `npm run typecheck` and `npm test`.

userStories.title: Generate P1 Quality Gate Report
userStories.description: As a project owner, I want a P1 quality report so that the team can decide whether the project is ready for P2.
acceptanceCriteria: Report includes rhythm metrics, event quality issue counts, format validation result, and condition expression test result
Report includes before/after comparison or baseline explanation
Report lists residual risks and P2/P3 candidate follow-ups
userStories.notes: Added aggregated P1 quality gate report generator `scripts/generateP1QualityGateReport.ts` with command `npm run report:p1-quality-gate`, producing `docs/test-reports/us-021-p1-quality-gate-report.md`. Report includes rhythm metrics snapshot, event quality issue counts, format validation result, condition expression check results, deterministic baseline explanation, and residual-risk/P2-P3 follow-up sections.

userStories.title: Close P1 with Verification Evidence
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want P1 closed with fresh validation evidence so that future sessions can continue safely.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run typecheck passes
npm run build passes
npm test passes
P1 quality command passes or emits approved waivers
Completed P1 stories have recorded verification evidence
userStories.notes: Closed P1 with fresh validation evidence in `docs/test-reports/us-022-p1-closure-verification.md`. Re-ran `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, `npm test`, and `npm run report:p1-quality-gate` (pass with `qualityGate=pass`, `blocker=0`, `major=69`, `minor=12`). Audited completed stories against `progress.txt` and confirmed all previously passed stories (`21/21`) have recorded evidence entries.
